title: CryptoCbGuard drop-before-Client invariant not visible at type level
description: The requirement that CryptoCbGuard must be dropped before Client is dropped is documented only in the register_cryptocb doc comment. Nothing at the CryptoCbGuard type or its Drop impl references this ordering. A contributor who stores both in a struct { client: Client, guard: CryptoCbGuard } gets use-after-free because Rust drops fields top-to-bottom and client is freed first. The Drop impl for CryptoCbGuard should carry a comment: 'SAFETY: caller must ensure Client is still alive when this guard is dropped; see register_cryptocb for ordering contract.' A #[must_use] on CryptoCbGuard is already implied by the RAII pattern but the struct should also carry a doc comment warning about the ordering requirement.
